text.title: stakeholder-register Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for stakeholder-register as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Including candid negative commentary about a stakeholder in a machine-readable field that is passed to third-party LLMs or shared in team channels.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Machine-readable candid data is one copy-paste or API call away from the subject; it damages trust, creates legal liability, and inhibits honest assessment in future registers.

failure-mode.description: Maintaining a single register with more than 50-100 entries without partitioning by category or org unit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Registers above 50 entries become unusable for active engagement planning; the PM dilutes attention across too many relationships. Partition by category (Sponsor, Regulator, User, Vendor) and operate on slices.
